agent_output:
{"executive_summary":"A food delivery platform connecting restaurants, customers, and delivery drivers was requested, but critical non-functional requirements, scale targets, integration boundaries, and business constraints are absent. Without these, recommending a specific architectural pattern (e.g., microservices vs modular monolith, event-driven vs request-response, real-time dispatch algorithm choice) would be speculative. This analysis captures the known domain structure and identifies every constraint that must be answered before architecture can be responsibly prescribed.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Customer Experience","responsibility":"Menu browsing, cart management, order placement, order tracking, ratings/reviews","boundaries":"Unclear — depends on whether this is a web app, mobile app, or both; and whether SSR or SPA"},{"name":"Restaurant Management","responsibility":"Menu/catalog management, order acceptance/rejection, preparation status updates, business hours, pricing","boundaries":"Unclear — self-service portal vs integrated POS vs both"},{"name":"Delivery/Logistics","responsibility":"Driver matching/dispatch, real-time location tracking, route optimization, delivery status","boundaries":"Unclear — in-house dispatch engine vs third-party logistics integration"},{"name":"Order Orchestration","responsibility":"Order lifecycle management, state machine transitions, payment coordination, refund/cancellation flows","boundaries":"Central coordination point — but whether synchronous saga or event-choreography is unknown without consistency requirements"},{"name":"Payment Processing","responsibility":"Payment capture, refund processing, restaurant payouts, driver payouts, tips","boundaries":"Unclear — which PSP, whether escrow model, payout frequency, multi-currency needs"},{"name":"Notifications","responsibility":"Push notifications, SMS, email for order updates, promotions, driver alerts","boundaries":"Unclear — real-time requirements, delivery guarantees, provider choices"},{"name":"Identity & Access","responsibility":"Authentication, authorization, role-based access for three actor types","boundaries":"Unclear — OAuth/OIDC provider, social login, phone-based auth for drivers"}],"interfaces":[],"data_flow":"Cannot be specified without knowing synchronous vs asynchronous communication preferences, consistency requirements, and whether the system is a monolith, modular monolith, or distributed services.","patterns_used":[]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Ordering","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order (lifecycle, line items, pricing snapshot, status transitions)","Cart (ephemeral, pre-order item collection)"]},{"name":"Catalog","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["Restaurant (profile, hours, delivery zones)","Menu (categories, items, modifiers, pricing, availability)"]},{"name":"Delivery","classification":"core","aggregates":["Delivery Assignment (driver-order pairing, status, location trail)","Driver (availability, current location, capacity)"]},{"name":"Payment","classification":"generic","aggregates":["Transaction (charge, refund, payout, ledger entries)"]},{"name":"Customer","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["Customer Profile (addresses, preferences, order history reference)","Rating/Review"]},{"name":"Promotion","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["Coupon/Discount (rules, validity, usage limits)"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Ordering","downstream":"Delivery","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Order context publishes OrderConfirmed; Delivery context subscribes to begin dispatch"},{"upstream":"Ordering","downstream":"Payment","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Order context requests payment capture/refund"},{"upstream":"Catalog","downstream":"Ordering","relationship":"Conformist — Ordering snapshots menu/pricing at order time to avoid drift"},{"upstream":"Delivery","downstream":"Ordering","relationship":"Event notification — Delivery publishes status updates consumed by Order for tracking"},{"upstream":"Promotion","downstream":"Ordering","relationship":"Shared Kernel or ACL — discount rules applied at order pricing time"}]},"trade_offs":[],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Dispatch Algorithm","evolution_path":"This is the highest-value flexibility point. The driver-matching algorithm (nearest driver, load balancing, predictive batching) will evolve continuously. It should be behind a strategy interface regardless of overall architecture."},{"area":"Payment Provider","evolution_path":"PSP integration should be behind an anti-corruption layer to allow provider switches (Stripe → Adyen, etc.) without domain impact."},{"area":"Notification Channels","evolution_path":"New channels (WhatsApp, in-app) will be added over time. A channel-agnostic notification dispatcher is warranted."},{"area":"Restaurant Integration","evolution_path":"Some restaurants will want POS integration, others a web portal. The catalog/order-acceptance interface must support multiple adapters."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Order State Machine","reason":"Order lifecycle transitions (placed → confirmed → preparing → ready → picked-up → delivered → completed/cancelled) must be strictly enforced. Invalid transitions cause financial and operational chaos."},{"area":"Payment Ledger","reason":"Financial records must be immutable and auditable. Append-only ledger pattern is non-negotiable regardless of other architectural choices."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Premature microservices decomposition without understanding team size, deployment constraints, and actual scale","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Answer scale and team-structure constraints before choosing deployment topology"},{"risk":"Real-time location tracking has fundamentally different infrastructure needs (high-frequency writes, geospatial queries) than order management — wrong tech choice here is expensive to fix","severity":"high","mitigation":"Clarify expected driver count, update frequency, and geo-query latency requirements before selecting data store"},{"risk":"Consistency model for order-payment-delivery coordination is the hardest architectural decision and cannot be made without knowing acceptable failure modes","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Define: what happens when payment succeeds but restaurant rejects? When driver cancels mid-delivery? These business rules dictate the architecture, not vice versa."},{"risk":"Multi-sided marketplace economics (commissions, surge pricing, promotions) create complex pricing logic that can become an unmaintainable tangle","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Isolate pricing/commission calculation as a dedicated domain service with clear inputs/outputs"}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"","files_to_create_or_modify":[],"patterns_to_follow":"","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do not choose microservices vs monolith without knowing team size and deployment constraints","Do not design the dispatch system without knowing expected driver/order concurrency","Do not pick a database without knowing read/write ratio, geospatial needs, and consistency requirements","Do not assume synchronous request-response — delivery tracking and notifications likely need event-driven patterns, but order placement may need synchronous consistency"]},"escalations":[{"type":"missing_constraints","description":"The following critical constraints are missing and MUST be answered before architecture can be prescribed:

1. **Scale targets**: Expected concurrent users, orders/hour, number of restaurants, number of drivers, geographic scope (single city vs multi-region vs global)
2. **Team size and structure**: How many engineers? One team or multiple? This is the #1 determinant of monolith vs services.
3. **Latency/availability NFRs**: What's acceptable order placement latency? Driver location update frequency? Tracking refresh rate?
4. **Consistency requirements**: Is eventual consistency acceptable for order status? Must payment be synchronously confirmed before order confirmation?
5. **Tech stack constraints**: Any existing infrastructure, language preferences, cloud provider commitments?
6. **Integration boundaries**: Restaurant POS integrations? Existing payment provider? Map/routing provider?
7. **Business rules for failure modes**: What happens when payment succeeds but restaurant is closed? Driver cancels mid-delivery? Customer cancels after food is prepared?
8. **Regulatory/compliance**: Food safety regulations, data residency, PCI compliance scope?
9. **Real-time requirements**: Is WebSocket/SSE needed for live tracking, or is polling acceptable?
10. **Multi-tenancy**: Single brand or white-label platform for multiple brands?","routed_to":"user","options":["Provide full constraint answers and I will deliver a complete architecture","Provide partial answers (e.g., 'early-stage startup, single city, small team') and I will scope the architecture appropriately","Choose a reference architecture tier: MVP (monolith, single city), Growth (modular monolith, multi-city), Scale (distributed services, multi-region)"],"recommendation":"Start by answering items 1, 2, 5, and 7 — these four alone eliminate 80% of architectural ambiguity. The domain model above is stable regardless of these answers and can be used as a starting point."}],"confidence":{"level":40,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain model and bounded context identification — food delivery is a well-understood domain","Core aggregates and their invariants","Flexibility and rigidity points","Risk identification"],"low_confidence_areas":["Deployment topology (monolith vs services)","Communication patterns (sync vs async vs hybrid)","Data store choices","Infrastructure and scaling approach","Specific interface contracts"],"assumptions":["This is a new system (greenfield), not a re-architecture of an existing platform","The platform is the marketplace operator, not a white-label solution","Standard three-sided marketplace model (customer, restaurant, driver)"]}}

content.thinking:
The user wants me to design an architecture for a food delivery platform. Let me classify this first.

Classification: `missing_constraints` — I have no NFRs, scale requirements, integration boundaries, tech stack preferences, or business constraints. "Food delivery platform connecting restaurants, customers, and delivery drivers" is extremely broad with no specifics.

I need to escalate this since I don't have enough constraints to make confident architectural decisions.

Without these, recommending a specific architectural pattern (e.g., microservices vs modular monolith, event-driven vs request-response, real-time dispatch algorithm choice) would be speculative. This analysis captures the known domain structure and identifies every constraint that must be answered before architecture can be responsibly availability)"]},{"name":"Delivery","classification":"core","aggregates":["Delivery Assignment (driver-order pairing, status, location trail)","Driver (availability, current location, capacity)"]},{"name":"Payment","classification":"generic","aggregates":["Transaction (charge, refund, payout, ledger entries)"]},{"name":"Customer","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["Customer Profile (addresses, preferences, order history reference)","Rating/Review"]},{"name":"Promotion","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["Coupon/Discount (rules, validity, usage limits)"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Ordering","downstream":"Delivery","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Order context publishes OrderConfirmed; Delivery context subscribes to begin dispatch"},{"upstream":"Ordering","downstream":"Payment","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Order context requests payment capture/refund"},{"upstream":"Catalog","downstream":"Ordering","relationship":"Conformist — Ordering snapshots menu/pricing at order time to avoid drift"},{"upstream":"Delivery","downstream":"Ordering","relationship":"Event notification — Delivery publishes status updates consumed by Order for tracking"},{"upstream":"Promotion","downstream":"Ordering","relationship":"Shared Kernel or ACL — discount rules applied at order pricing The driver-matching algorithm (nearest driver, load balancing, predictive batching) will evolve continuously. It should be behind a strategy interface regardless of overall architecture."},{"area":"Payment Provider","evolution_path":"PSP integration should be behind an anti-corruption layer to allow provider switches (Stripe → Adyen, etc.) without domain impact."},{"area":"Notification Channels","evolution_path":"New channels (WhatsApp, in-app) will be added over time. A channel-agnostic notification dispatcher is warranted."},{"area":"Restaurant Integration","evolution_path":"Some restaurants will want POS integration, others a web portal. Invalid transitions cause financial and operational chaos."},{"area":"Payment Ledger","reason":"Financial records must be immutable and auditable. Append-only ledger pattern is non-negotiable regardless of other architectural choices."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Premature microservices decomposition without understanding team size, deployment constraints, and actual scale","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Answer scale and team-structure constraints before choosing deployment topology"},{"risk":"Real-time location tracking has fundamentally different infrastructure needs (high-frequency writes, geospatial queries) than order management — wrong tech choice here is expensive to fix","severity":"high","mitigation":"Clarify expected driver count, update frequency, and geo-query latency requirements before selecting data store"},{"risk":"Consistency model for order-payment-delivery coordination is the hardest architectural decision and cannot be made without knowing acceptable failure modes","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Define: what happens when payment succeeds but restaurant rejects? When driver cancels mid-delivery? These business rules dictate the architecture, not vice versa."},{"risk":"Multi-sided marketplace economics (commissions, surge pricing, promotions) create complex pricing logic that can become an unmaintainable tangle","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Isolate pricing/commission calculation as a dedicated domain service with clear inputs/outputs"}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"","files_to_create_or_modify":[],"patterns_to_follow":"","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do not choose microservices vs monolith without knowing team size and deployment constraints","Do not design the dispatch system without knowing expected driver/order concurrency","Do not pick a database without knowing read/write ratio, geospatial needs, and consistency requirements","Do not assume synchronous request-response — delivery tracking and notifications likely need event-driven patterns, but order placement may need synchronous consistency"]},"escalations":[{"type":"missing_constraints","description":"The following critical constraints are missing and MUST be answered before architecture can be prescribed:

1. **Scale targets**: Expected concurrent users, orders/hour, number of restaurants, number of drivers, geographic scope (single city vs multi-region vs global)
2. **Team size and structure**: How many engineers? One team or multiple? This is the #1 determinant of monolith vs services.
3. **Latency/availability NFRs**: What's acceptable order placement latency? Driver location update frequency? Tracking refresh rate?
4. **Consistency requirements**: Is eventual consistency acceptable for order status? Must payment be synchronously confirmed before order confirmation?
5. **Tech stack constraints**: Any existing infrastructure, language preferences, cloud provider commitments?
6. **Integration boundaries**: Restaurant POS integrations? Existing payment provider? Map/routing provider?
7. **Business rules for failure modes**: What happens when payment succeeds but restaurant is closed? Driver cancels mid-delivery? Customer cancels after food is prepared?
8. **Regulatory/compliance**: Food safety regulations, data residency, PCI compliance scope?
9. **Real-time requirements**: Is WebSocket/SSE needed for live tracking, or is polling acceptable?
10. **Multi-tenancy**: Single brand or white-label platform for multiple brands?","routed_to":"user","options":["Provide full constraint answers and I will deliver a complete architecture","Provide partial answers (e.g., 'early-stage startup, single city, small team') and I will scope the architecture appropriately","Choose a reference architecture tier: MVP (monolith, single city), Growth (modular monolith, multi-city), Scale (distributed services, multi-region)"],"recommendation":"Start by answering items 1, 2, 5, and 7 — these four alone eliminate 80% of architectural ambiguity. The domain model above is stable regardless of these answers and can be used as a starting point."}],"confidence":{"level":40,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain model and bounded context identification — food delivery is a well-understood domain","Core aggregates and their invariants","Flexibility and rigidity points","Risk identification"],"low_confidence_areas":["Deployment topology (monolith vs services)","Communication patterns (sync vs async vs hybrid)","Data store choices","Infrastructure and scaling approach","Specific interface contracts"],"assumptions":["This is a new system (greenfield), not a re-architecture of an existing platform","The platform is the marketplace operator, not a white-label solution","Standard three-sided marketplace model (customer, restaurant, driver)"]}}
